assertion_results.text: A SKILL.md file exists in the output skill directory
The SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
assertion_results.evidence: Frontmatter contains 'name: git-commit-helper' and 'description: Analyze staged git changes and generate conventional commit messages...' between --- delimiters on lines 1-4

assertion_results.text: The name field uses only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
assertion_results.evidence: name is 'git-commit-helper' — only lowercase letters and hyphens, no uppercase, spaces, or special characters

assertion_results.text: The description includes 'Use when' trigger conditions
assertion_results.evidence: Description contains literal 'Use when' on line 3: '...Use when writing commit messages, committing code, preparing git commits, generating changelogs...'

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body contains a numbered workflow or step-by-step instructions
assertion_results.evidence: Workflow section contains 5 numbered steps: '### 1. Gather staged changes', '### 2. Determine commit type', '### 3. Detect monorepo scope', '### 4. Compose the commit message', '### 5. Present and confirm'

assertion_results.text: A scripts/ directory exists with at least one Bun TypeScript script (.ts file)
assertion_results.evidence: scripts/ directory contains parse-diff.ts — a Bun TypeScript file with shebang '#!/usr/bin/env bun' (393 lines)

assertion_results.text: The script includes a --help flag or usage documentation
assertion_results.evidence: parse-diff.ts checks for --help on line 56: 'if (args.includes("--help"))' and prints comprehensive HELP text (lines 16-50) covering usage, options, output format, exit codes, and examples

assertion_results.text: An evals/evals.json file exists with at least 2 test cases
assertion_results.evidence: evals/evals.json exists with 3 test cases (ids 1-3): auth middleware commit, monorepo bug fix commit, and docs-only commit

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body includes a Common mistakes or pitfalls section
assertion_results.evidence: Section '## Common mistakes' on line 167 with a table of 8 mistake/fix pairs covering tense, capitalization, scope, type selection, subject focus, length, periods, and monorepo scopes

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body is under 500 lines
assertion_results.evidence: SKILL.md is 200 lines, well under the 500-line limit

assertion_results.text: The script uses structured JSON output to stdout
assertion_results.evidence: parse-diff.ts uses JSON.stringify on line 318 (empty result) and line 384 (main result): 'console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2))' outputting a structured DiffResult object with files, stats, suggested_type, suggested_scope, type_reasoning, is_monorepo, and diff_summary fields
